tutorial_ruins_invitation.name: Adventurer
lines: Hey! I haven't seen you here before.
Are you a new adventurer? If you are, you should totally explore the ruins to my right.
The rest of my party is already inside. I think they would be willing to help you if you need it.

tutorial_ruins_about_elevator.name: Adventurer
lines: What am I doing? I am trying to analyze this ancient lamp.
These old contraptions truly are interesting...
That elevator for example; how does it still work after all this time?

tutorial_ruins_deploy_puzzle_hint.name: Adventurer
lines: You want to know my thoughts on this puzzle? It certainly is interesting.
Based on what I can tell, you need to place a robot onto one of the buttons to open the first door.
To achieve this, you can open your inventory (Tab), select your robot and deploy it.
The door after it then only opens when there are no robots on any of the buttons.
If you configure the robot (E while near it), you can destroy it and pick it back up.

tutorial_ruins_first_program_puzzle_hint.name: Adventurer
lines: It looks like here we only need to move a robot onto the button.
However, we are only allowed to deploy robots in the area shown by the hammers.
I guess that means we have to program the robot to move onto the button.
Once you have a robot deployed, you configure it (E while near it) and use left click to add a processor to the modules.

You can then program the robot (C while near it) and write:

move(right)
Then you just need to start the robot, possibly several times.
Don't forget to take the robot with you when you're done!

tutorial_ruins_loop_puzzle_hint.name: Adventurer
lines: This puzzle looks similar to the previous one, doesn't it?
Looks can be deceiving though. See those red antennas? Your robot cannot stop inside of that area.

tutorial_ruins_follow_puzzle_hint.name: Adventurer
lines: For this puzzle the robot needs to move for a very long time without stopping.
If only there was a way for the robot to follow the player?

I think the robot would move towards the player if one wrote something like this:

move(playerDir())
Maybe this could be combined with a loop?

merchant_greeting_1.name: Merchant
lines: Anything that catches your eye?
@OPEN_SHOP:spawn_town

merchant_greeting_2.name: Merchant
lines: Do you have something specific in mind, or are you just browsing?
@OPEN_SHOP:spawn_town

combat_ruins_warning.name: Adventurer
lines: Hey, are you really sure you wanna go in there?
Be prepared to fight a lot of uncontrolled robots...
